title: Slater-Usoh-Steed (SUS) Presence Questionnaire
description: 6-item ordinal scale measuring the subjective sense of 'being there' in a virtual environment. Designed for brevity and high sensitivity to HMD/tracking quality differences.
citation: Slater, M., Usoh, M., & Steed, A. (1994). Depth of presence in virtual environments. Presence, 3(2), 130-144. | Usoh, M., Catena, E., Arman, S., & Slater, M. (2000). Using presence questionnaires in reality. Presence, 9(5), 497-503.

SUS1.text: To what extent do you feel that you visited the virtual place rather than just saw images of it?

SUS2.text: To what extent were there times during the experience when the virtual place was the reality for you?

SUS3.text: When you think back about your experience, do you think of the virtual place more as images you saw or more as somewhere you visited?

SUS4.text: During the experience, did you often think to yourself that you were actually in the virtual environment?

SUS5.text: How much did the virtual environment seem real to you?

SUS6.text: How compelling was your sense of moving around inside the virtual environment?

scoring.description: Count the number of items rated >= 6 (on a 1-7 scale). Presence is considered high if >= 3 items score >= 6.
scoring.formula: SUS_score = mean(SUS1, SUS2, SUS3, SUS4, SUS5, SUS6)
scoring.note: The threshold method is more commonly reported in Slater lab papers; mean is used in comparative studies.

strengths: Sensitive to hardware quality differences (FOV, tracking latency)
limitations: Does not decompose presence into subscales
Less diagnostic than IPQ or ITC-SOPI
